forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a480
+title: Up/down card nav lands on an inner scope of the target card, not the card
+---
+Symptom: focused on a board card, pressing up moves focus into the \"middle of the card above\" (an inner scope of that card) instead of the card itself.
+
+Mechanism: cardinal nav (navigate.rs) is purely geometric over ALL snapshot scopes with a \"leaves win over containers\" tie-break (better_candidate). A card is a container (has inner scopes: inspect button + likely an inspectable/body scope with ~the same rect). When the target card and an inner leaf score equal/close, the leaf wins → focus lands inside the card above.
+
+NOT introduced this session: navigate.rs is unchanged except workspace-move commits; the focus-indicator union fix (focus-scope.tsx) just made focus visible so the landing is now observable.
+
+Investigate: entity-card.tsx + inspectable.tsx scope structure — is there a redundant inner scope sharing the card's rect (needless-nesting)? Decide fix at the right layer:
+- (a) remove the redundant inner same-rect scope so the card is the sole target, or
+- (b) refine the leaf-over-container tie-break so a leaf that is a DESCENDANT of a tying container does not steal the pick from a real (showFocus=true) card, while preserving the board/column (showFocus=false) container→card behavior the tie-break was added for.
+
+Guard with a navigate.rs unit test (card-above with inner same-rect leaf → up picks the card) and a board-view spatial test.
+
+#bug #focus #spatial-nav #navigation
\ No newline at end of file

-- (b) refine the leaf-over-container tie-break so a leaf that is a DESCENDANT of a tying container does not steal the pick from a real (showFocus=true) card, while preserving the board/column (showFocus=false) container→card behavior the tie-break was added for.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,16 +5,18 @@
 position_ordinal: '80'
 title: Up/down card nav lands on an inner scope of the target card, not the card
 ---
-Symptom: focused on a board card, pressing up moves focus into the \"middle of the card above\" (an inner scope of that card) instead of the card itself.
+RESOLVED (kernel). Symptom: Up/Down on a board card jumped into a field in the middle of the card above instead of the card.
+
+Root cause: cardinal nav (navigate.rs::geometric_pick) is purely geometric over ALL snapshot scopes. A short card's title field shares ~the card's rect, so the leaf-over-container tie-break picked the inner field over the card.
+
+Fix (navigate.rs): in geometric_pick, drop a candidate that descends from another candidate which is a SIBLING of the focused scope (its parent_zone == focused.parent_zone), and skip ancestors of the focused scope. The card above is a sibling of the focused card (same column) → its title/inspect descendants are dropped, card wins. Next column's card is not a sibling → cross-column Left/Right still lands on it by beam score. A field's siblings are the other fields of the same card → within-card Up/Down unaffected. No snapshot schema change needed (tried/rejected a `focusable` flag: columns are showFocus=true so it can't separate column from card).

-Mechanism: cardinal nav (navigate.rs) is purely geometric over ALL snapshot scopes with a \"leaves win over containers\" tie-break (better_candidate). A card is a container (has inner scopes: inspect button + likely an inspectable/body scope with ~the same rect). When the target card and an inner leaf score equal/close, the leaf wins → focus lands inside the card above.
+Tests: added authoritative navigate.rs unit tests for cards+nested fields (Up/Down between cards → card not field; Down inside a card → that card's fields; Right → next column's card not its title). Existing cross-zone + parent_zone-cycle tests pass unchanged. cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus: 64/64.

-NOT introduced this session: navigate.rs is unchanged except workspace-move commits; the focus-indicator union fix (focus-scope.tsx) just made focus visible so the landing is now observable.
+Commits on plugin: 2a05bc4ca (pushed 5ee00e204..2a05bc4ca).

-Investigate: entity-card.tsx + inspectable.tsx scope structure — is there a redundant inner scope sharing the card's rect (needless-nesting)? Decide fix at the right layer:
-- (a) remove the redundant inner same-rect scope so the card is the sole target, or
-- (b) refine the leaf-over-container tie-break so a leaf that is a DESCENDANT of a tying container does not steal the pick from a real (showFocus=true) card, while preserving the board/column (showFocus=false) container→card behavior the tie-break was added for.
+WHY TESTS MISSED IT — important follow-up (new task worthy): the JS port apps/kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts::navigateInShadow is a SIBLING-SCOPED cascade and references Rust fns (beam_among_in_zone_any_kind/pick_best_candidate) that no longer exist — it diverged from the rewritten geometric kernel. The UI spatial tests validate that phantom port, not the kernel, so the sibling-scoped port never dives into fields and the regression passed every UI test. navigate.rs unit tests are the source of truth. The JS port should be rewritten to mirror geometric_pick (or replaced with a wasm binding). See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.

-Guard with a navigate.rs unit test (card-above with inner same-rect leaf → up picks the card) and a board-view spatial test.
+Needs user verification in the real app: Up/Down moves card→card; cross-column Left/Right lands on a card; once inside a card, Up/Down walks that card's fields.

 #bug #focus #spatial-nav #navigation
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,18 +5,18 @@
 position_ordinal: '8280'
 title: Up/down card nav lands on an inner scope of the target card, not the card
 ---
-RESOLVED (kernel). Symptom: Up/Down on a board card jumped into a field in the middle of the card above instead of the card.
+RESOLVED (kernel) + zone-skip enhancement. Symptom: Up/Down on a board card jumped into a field in the middle of the card above instead of the card.

-Root cause: cardinal nav (navigate.rs::geometric_pick) is purely geometric over ALL snapshot scopes. A short card's title field shares ~the card's rect, so the leaf-over-container tie-break picked the inner field over the card.
+Fix 1 (commit 2a05bc4ca): geometric_pick drops a candidate that descends from another candidate which is a SIBLING of the focused scope (same parent_zone); skips ancestors of focused. Card above is a sibling → its fields dropped, card wins. Cross-column still lands on the next card by beam score. Within-card field nav unaffected.

-Fix (navigate.rs): in geometric_pick, drop a candidate that descends from another candidate which is a SIBLING of the focused scope (its parent_zone == focused.parent_zone), and skip ancestors of the focused scope. The card above is a sibling of the focused card (same column) → its title/inspect descendants are dropped, card wins. Next column's card is not a sibling → cross-column Left/Right still lands on it by beam score. A field's siblings are the other fields of the same card → within-card Up/Down unaffected. No snapshot schema change needed (tried/rejected a `focusable` flag: columns are showFocus=true so it can't separate column from card).
+Fix 2 / addressed the flagged edge (commit 6744a834e): added SnapshotScope.focusable (mirrors <FocusScope showFocus>, serde-default true; React buildSnapshot sets it from the registry entry). geometric_pick skips non-focusable scopes as cardinal candidates, so a move passes through a structural zone (board well, perspective bar — showFocus=false) and lands on the focusable child beneath instead of the indicator-less zone. NOTE: this zone-landing was pre-existing (the zone was already the lowest-beam-score candidate), not a regression from Fix 1.

-Tests: added authoritative navigate.rs unit tests for cards+nested fields (Up/Down between cards → card not field; Down inside a card → that card's fields; Right → next column's card not its title). Existing cross-zone + parent_zone-cycle tests pass unchanged. cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus: 64/64.
+Tests: navigate.rs unit tests for cards+nested fields (up/down→card not field; within-card→fields; cross-column→card not title; down-from-navbar→card not board well). focus-crate 65 lib + all integration green; tsc clean; 80 focus/nav UI tests pass; kanban-app compiles.

-Commits on plugin: 2a05bc4ca (pushed 5ee00e204..2a05bc4ca).
+Commits on plugin: 2a05bc4ca, 6744a834e (pushed). Files: navigate.rs, snapshot.rs (+focusable field), state.rs, registry.rs, focus integration tests, commands.rs, types/spatial.ts, layer-scope-registry-context.tsx, focus-scope.tsx.

-WHY TESTS MISSED IT — important follow-up (new task worthy): the JS port apps/kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts::navigateInShadow is a SIBLING-SCOPED cascade and references Rust fns (beam_among_in_zone_any_kind/pick_best_candidate) that no longer exist — it diverged from the rewritten geometric kernel. The UI spatial tests validate that phantom port, not the kernel, so the sibling-scoped port never dives into fields and the regression passed every UI test. navigate.rs unit tests are the source of truth. The JS port should be rewritten to mirror geometric_pick (or replaced with a wasm binding). See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.
+Needs user verification in app: up/down card↔card; cross-column left/right→card; inside a card up/down→fields; arrow from nav bar → a card (not the invisible board well).

-Needs user verification in the real app: Up/Down moves card→card; cross-column Left/Right lands on a card; once inside a card, Up/Down walks that card's fields.
+Follow-up still open: 01KST708RZ (reconcile diverged JS nav port so UI tests actually validate the kernel).

 #bug #focus #spatial-nav #navigation
\ No newline at end of file
